body.title: Fix stale test_no_gate_wired_skips_check for cold-start fail-closed
body.result: already shipped 440fa2fd — renamed test + cold-start fail-closed assertion
body.intention: I intend that the policy gate test suite correctly documents the cold-start fail-closed contract so no reader mistakes the deny behavior for a bug.
body.description:
test_no_gate_wired_skips_check expects dispatch to succeed when _policy_gate is None — but the shim now intentionally raises PolicyDeniedError in that case (cold-start fail-closed, see shim.py comment). The test documents the old, less-secure behavior and needs updating to assert the deny.

**Affected files:** `tests/test_shim_policy_gate.py` (one test method + test name)
**Design rules:** none apply
**Scope boundary:** update the one test method and its name to match current behavior; do not change shim logic.
**Completion criteria:** `test_shim_policy_gate.py` passes green; test name updated to `test_no_gate_wired_fails_closed`; assertion changed to `pytest.raises(PolicyDeniedError)`.
